items.title: New Blazers coach Micah Nori didn't just answer questions at his introductory press conference. He attacked them with a fast-talking passion and Southern twang, befitting of his reputation as the Ted Lasso of the NBA.
items.author: Conor Bergin
items.body_excerpt:
New Blazers coach Micah Nori didn't just answer questions at his introductory press conference. He attacked them with a fast-talking passion and Southern twang, befitting of his reputation as the Ted Lasso of the NBA.

He arrives with the personality and experience to bring a jolt to Rip City.

media.title: The Micah Nori Era Begins With a Jolt of Energy
media.description: The new coach introduced himself to Rip City by nailing his first press conference.

items.title: It is true that OKC is cutting costs. Literally a fact. We'll see if any other moves are made to further reduce costs, which seems likely.
items.author: Jon Hamm
items.body_excerpt:
It is true that OKC is cutting costs. Literally a fact. We'll see if any other moves are made to further reduce costs, which seems likely.

But usually when teams dump salary, they are filling those roster spots with vet minimums or fringe 2nd rounders. OKC is filling with 1st round draft talent.

items.title: Only the Pistons can offer Jalen Duren five years and up to $287 million. Even in a sign-and-trade, outside teams are capped at four years and up to $177.4 million.
items.author: Omari Sankofa II
items.body_excerpt:
Only the Pistons can offer Jalen Duren five years and up to $287 million. Even in a sign-and-trade, outside teams are capped at four years and up to $177.4 million.

But a sign-and-trade also would allow more teams to get in the mix. www.freep.com/story/sports...

items.title: By moving Isaiah Joe without taking salary back, the Oklahoma City Thunder now projects to be $14.8M under the luxury tax, $22.8M under the first apron and $35.8M under the second apron.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
By moving Isaiah Joe without taking salary back, the Oklahoma City Thunder now projects to be $14.8M under the luxury tax, $22.8M under the first apron and $35.8M under the second apron.

This is without anything on the books for Lu Dort, Isaiah Hartenstein or Kenrich Williams. OKC creating 2A room.

items.title: Two second-rounders for Isaiah Joe was the exact deal I wanted the Bulls to make. Great work for the Pistons, feels like he could have a similar impact to what Malik Beasley did there a couple years ago.
items.author: Ricky O'Donnell
items.body_excerpt: Two second-rounders for Isaiah Joe was the exact deal I wanted the Bulls to make. Great work for the Pistons, feels like he could have a similar impact to what Malik Beasley did there a couple years ago.

items.title: How the Pistons acquire Isaiah Joe is TBD.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
How the Pistons acquire Isaiah Joe is TBD.

They could use their $15 million tax mid-level or expand the Isaiah Stewart trade to acquire him.

They could also still create as much as $34 million in cap space to absorb Joe while retaining Jalen Duren's Bird rights.
